steps.title: Establish the growth rate in months
steps.content:
The tree grows 87 cm every $m$ months. The rate per month is:
$$\text{rate} = \frac{87}{m} \text{ cm/month}$$

steps.title: Convert k years to months
steps.content:
Since there are 12 months per year:
$$k \text{ years} = 12k \text{ months}$$

steps.title: Multiply rate by time to get total growth
Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: $\dfrac{29m}{4k}$ — rate and time are inverted; growth should increase with $k$, not decrease. Eliminated.
  • B: $\dfrac{29k}{4m}$ — $29/4 = 7.25$, not $87 \times 12 / \text{something}$; coefficient is wrong. Eliminated.
  • C: $\dfrac{1{,}044m}{k}$ — $m$ and $k$ positions are swapped; growth should be proportional to $k$ and inversely proportional to $m$. Eliminated.
  • D: $\dfrac{1{,}044k}{m}$ — coefficient $87 \times 12 = 1{,}044$, numerator $k$ (more years = more growth), denominator $m$ (larger $m$ means slower monthly rate). Matches exactly.
D is correct.
